description: Pinacoteca di Brera (Milan) golden search cases (D13, 10): the per-museum accuracy north-star for the Brera rows in the merged artifact. Authored 2026-07-06 against the full 637-record wp-json/wp/v2/opera harvest (356 on-view rows after the non-esposte/no-room/off-site filters — see sources/brera.ts) + the DeepSeek it->en translate.ts pass (353/356 rows carry titleAlt). Titles are Italian (title = source language; translateFrom: "it"). Per run-goldens.mjs's matches(): expectTitleContains checks the returned r.title field ONLY (which stays Italian, never titleAlt) — so the Italian-query cases below assert expectTitleContains in Italian, while the English-query cases (which only match via the FTS index's titleAlt half, not the displayed title) assert expectObjectIDs instead, same convention the SMK/Rijksmuseum sets use for translated titles. objectIDs looked up live from the built met.sqlite (48-bit hash of museum/sourceId, stable as long as sourceId — the WP numeric post id — doesn't change). Tiers mirror the Met set: autocomplete = local FTS top-8; full = all-results; llm = relaxed-FTS stand-in for the interpret tier.

cases.note: Hayez's The Kiss, sourceId 526, objectID 210394956009769, on view room XXXVIII. Italian-language query against the Italian title.
Caravaggio's Supper at Emmaus, sourceId 788, objectID 182522163816540, on view room XXVIII. Italian query, artist + title tokens.
Raphael's Marriage of the Virgin, sourceId 550, objectID 184983150356654, on view room XXIV. Italian query.
Mantegna's Cristo morto nel sepolcro e tre dolenti (the Lamentation over the Dead Christ), sourceId 594, objectID 112709237292906, on view room VI. Italian query.
Hayez's Melancholy, sourceId 528, objectID 218115357092741, on view room XXXVII. Italian query, a less-famous icon to exercise a second artist/title pair.

English-for-Italian: 'The Kiss' only matches via the FTS index's titleAlt half (r.title stays 'Il bacio' Italian) — asserted by objectID, not expectTitleContains, per the run-goldens.mjs matches() convention (same as the Rijksmuseum/SMK translated-title cases).
English-for-Italian titleAlt case for Caravaggio's Cena in Emmaus; artist token in English + English title tokens, strict-AND across the translated titleAlt column.

English-for-Italian titleAlt case for Raphael's Sposalizio della Vergine. NOT '... raphael': translate.ts only Englishes title/facet columns, not artist — the artist column stays the Italian 'Raffaello Sanzio', so an English 'raphael' token has no searchable source and zeroes the strict-AND tier (same documented gap as V&A's silver-medium case). This title is shared with 5 other museums' same-subject works (Met, NGA, Louvre, V&A) — the Brera row lands 6th of 8 in the autocomplete top-8, which is enough for matches()'s `.some()` check.

cases.query: a couple in a passionate embrace kissing on a staircase
cases.note: Descriptive phrasing for Hayez's The Kiss; needs the relaxed-FTS stand-in to survive translation (no single anchor token overlaps 'Il bacio' directly, unlike the Rijksmuseum straatje case).

cases.query: a caravaggio supper scene where christ reveals his identity
cases.note: Descriptive phrasing for Caravaggio's Supper at Emmaus, keeping the artist token (same technique as the V&A/Rijksmuseum llm-tier cases) plus 'supper' as a literal anchor overlapping the translated titleAlt — purely generic Passion-narrative phrasing without an artist/anchor token ranked several Christ-themed Met/NGA/rijksmuseum rows above this one instead (measured live 2026-07-06).
